channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ontario slaps 25% increase on electricity exports to U.S. in response to Trump's trade war
item.description: Trump launched a new trade war last week by imposing tariffs against Washington’s three biggest trading partners, drawing immediate retaliation from Mexico, Canada and China and sending financial markets into a tailspin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Americans have clear opinions on Trump's performance in his first 50 days
item.description: As President Donald Trump marks 50 days into his second tour of duty in the White House, the most recent polls suggest that Americans are divided on the job he's doing
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: The Education Department’s Office for Civil Rights is best known for enforcing disability rights across America’s schools, but under President Donald Trump it’s being reshaped into a frontline figure in the president’s political battles.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Monday the Trump administration had finished its six-week purge of programs of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development and he would move the 18% of aid and development programs that survived under the State Department.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DC begins removing 'Black Lives Matter' plaza from street near White House
item.description: Crews have begun work to remove the large yellow “Black Lives Matter” painted on the street one block from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: "Gross abuses of executive power raise serious concerns over the freedoms of peaceful assembly, expression and association," the group wrote of the U.S. rating.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon losing cutting edge on weapons innovation, needs 'massive kick in the pants,' say defense leaders
item.description: America’s defenses will not be able to keep up with its adversaries if the Pentagon continues to take years to innovate weapons systems, experts agreed at a security summit last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Trump downplays tariff concerns as US stock sell-off worsens
item.description: President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices and isn’t ruling out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Air Force intercepts aircraft flying in a restricted zone near Mar-a-Lago
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Air Force fighter jets intercepted a civilian aircraft flying in the temporarily restricted airspace near Donald Trump's Florida home Sunday, bringing the number of violations to more than 20 since the president took office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump downplays business concerns about uncertainty from his tariffs and prospect of higher prices
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices, and isn't ruling out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump DOJ's limits on FACE Act enforcement fuel concern from abortion providers
item.description: New Justice Department leaders say past enforcement of the Freedom of Access to Clinic Entrances Act is "the prototypical example" of what they call "the weaponization of law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: DENVER (AP) — For decades, conservatives in Congress have talked about the need to cut government deeply, but they have always pulled back from mandating specific reductions, fearful of voter backlash.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance ramps up his clout in Trump world
item.description: Vice President Vance’s clout is on the rise, taking on the role of pot stirrer at the White House and on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump shifts tone on Musk as tensions rise with Cabinet
item.description: President Trump is signaling a softer approach to the mass firings of federal government workers led by Elon Musk as cracks emerge between the tech billionaire and Cabinet secretaries running the agencies he’s gutting.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman: 'Columbia pays for its failure' with $400M in grants axed
item.description: The campus was a hot spot for pro-Palestine protests after the Hamas attack on Oct. 7, 2023, sparked discourse in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New EPA guidance says spending items greater than $50,000 must get approval from DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency has issued new guidance directing that spending items greater than $50,000 now require approval from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada lawmaker proposes searchable domestic violence offender database
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would create a searchable database to see if someone has multiple domestic violence-related convictions to their name.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump admin investigation finds Maine Dept. of Education violated Title IX
item.description: On Feb. 25, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services sent a notice of violation against the Maine Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Students rally at Florida capitol opposing amendment petition reform
item.description: From the Panhandle to the Keys, hundreds of students from across Florida protested Thursday at the state capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump limits Musk's power as federal workforce concerns grow
item.description: While new changes to the federal workforce may be limited, Musk's orders have already reshaped several federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25 percent tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
